name: PaddyShack Golf & Game Lounge
address: 201 S Main St, Fort Atkinson, WI 53538
about: PaddyShack Golf & Game Lounge is a family-owned venue in Fort Atkinson that combines golf simulation with a social bar and arcade atmosphere. They feature state-of-the-art TrackMan golf simulators offering an immersive experience with access to world-famous courses. The venue provides entertainment for all ages with a mix of golf, arcade games, and a lounge area. It is available for both casual play and private events, including corporate gatherings and celebrations. Pricing is structured on a per-bay basis, with sessions available in half-hour increments through their online booking system.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to premium TrackMan simulators with course-play technology
A social environment combining golf with arcade games and a lounge
All-weather indoor golf experience available year-round
Half-hour booking increments for flexible session lengths
Available for both casual play and serious practice

food_and_drink.notes: Full kitchen available with a dedicated food menu accessible via their website.
Well-stocked bar featuring various bourbons and brews.

simulator_hardware.notes: State-of-the-art TrackMan golf simulators installed for an immersive experience.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify club rentals, so it is recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: PaddyShack has a full kitchen and bar on-site, serving food and drinks, but their policy on outside food and beverages is not specified.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: With half-hour booking increments, the time to play 18 holes can vary depending on the simulator software and player pace, but it is typically estimated to be around 2-3 hours.

faq.question: Is PaddyShack kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, PaddyShack is designed to be a family-friendly venue offering entertainment for all ages, including arcade games and a social atmosphere.
